title: Micromeles lanata
taxonomy: Micromeles lanata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rhamnaceae, commonly known as the buckthorn or dogwood family. This family is part of the larger order Rosales. The Rhamnaceae family consists of approximately 1,000 species spread across 70 genera. The exact classification of Micromeles lanata is further refined to the genus level, indicating its unique characteristics and distinctiveness within the Rhamnaceae family. The comprehensive classification of Micromeles lanata is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Rosids, Order: Rosales, Family: Rhamnaceae, Genus: Micromeles, Species: M. lanata.
uses: Micromeles lanata, a plant known for its potential medicinal properties, is used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. Its extracts have been found to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The plant's leaves and stems are said to have been used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. benefits: Micromeles lanata, a plant native to the Himalayas, has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its potential health benefits. intro: Micromeles lanata, also known as the Himalayan silver berry or Himalayan silver leaf, is a plant species native to the Himalayan region, classified as a type of flowering plant in the family Rhamnaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in the treatment of various health conditions, such as digestive issues and infections. The bioactive constituents of Micromeles lanata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal teas, extracts, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of health issues, and it is also used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine.
